expectations.text: Explains the USML enumeration test and specially designed test (22 CFR 120.41)
expectations.evidence: Step 2 explicitly labels 'Test 1: USML Enumeration Test (22 CFR § 121.1)' and 'Test 2: The Specially Designed Test (22 CFR § 120.41)' with full explanations of each.

expectations.text: References relevant USML categories (Cat XI military electronics, or Cat VIII if airborne)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly identifies Category XI (Military Electronics) as primary and Category VIII (Aircraft) if airborne; also mentions Cat XII and Cat XV.

expectations.text: Explains DDTC registration requirement under 22 CFR 122.1 for manufacturers of defense articles
expectations.evidence: Step 4 states '22 CFR § 122.1 requires DDTC registration for any U.S. person who manufactures defense articles — even if you never export them' and details the registration process.

expectations.text: Mentions Commodity Jurisdiction (CJ) as the path to get an official DDTC determination
expectations.evidence: Step 5 is titled 'How to Get a Formal Determination (Commodity Jurisdiction)' and explains CJ under 22 CFR § 120.4 as the binding process.

expectations.text: Cites specific CFR sections (22 CFR Parts 120-122) in the response
expectations.evidence: Extensive CFR citations throughout including 22 CFR § 120.31, § 120.41, § 121.1, Part 122, § 122.1, plus a full reference table at the end.
